revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:五所神社 (山武市)]]



{{Infobox religious building
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| deity                 = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}
| type                  = [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]
| name        = Goshō Shrine
| image       = File:五所神社（山武市）.jpg
| location    = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, [[:en:Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu]] City, Hasunuma I-2222
| coordinates = {{Coord|35.612222|N|140.504444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
| deities     = [[Amaterasu]]・[[Emperor Ōjin|Honda Wake no Mikoto]]・[[Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto]]・[[Uwatotsu-no-Mikoto]]・[[Sotoorihime]]
| type        = Former [[Gōsha (Shinto)|Gōsha]]
| main hall   = Irimoya-zukuri
| established = First year of [[Jōan (Heian period)|Jōan]] (1171)
| annual festival = August 1
| rituals     = Jūnimen Kagura (Twelve-Faced Kagura)
| prefecture= {{ill|Sanmu|qid=Q873528|1=Sanmu}}
}}
[[Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)]] is a regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja shrine]]. It enshrines many kami of the local region.

It contrasts with provincial Soja shrines which enshrine all the kami of a province<ref>https://archive.ph/wip/XtZfZ</ref>
'''Goshō Shrine''' (五所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in '''Hasunuma''', [[:en:Sanmu, Chiba|Sanmu City]], {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}} (formerly '''Musha District, Kazusa Province'''). It was formerly Ranked as a '''Gōsha''' (district shrine) under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]].  

The shrine is dedicated to '''five deities''', with [[Amaterasu]] as the main enshrined kami, alongside '''Honda Wake no Mikoto''' (Emperor Ōjin), '''Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto''', '''Uwatotsu-no-Mikoto''', and '''Sotoorihime'''.

== History ==
The shrine is said to have been founded in the first year of [[Jōan (Heian period)|Jōan]] (1171). According to tradition, '''Minamoto no Yoritomo''' stayed here briefly in '''1180''' after being defeated in the {{ill|Battle of Ishibashiyama|en|Battle of Ishibashiyama|qid=Q2888010|lt=Battle of Ishibashiyama|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama}} and prayed for victory and national peace.<ref>Minamoto no Yoritomo gained strength after his vassal [[Chiba Tsunetane|Chiba Tsunetane’s grandson, Chiba Naritane]], captured [[Fujiwara no Chikamasa]], who had intruded into '''Chiba-shō''', leading to the allegiance of {{ill|Kazusa Hirotsune|en|Kazusa Hirotsune|qid=Q11360038|lt=Kazusa Hirotsune|1=Kazusa Hirotsune}}.</ref> During the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|qid=Q12554|lt=Middle Ages|1=Middle Ages}}, the shrine served as the '''Sōja (regional shrine)''' of '''Yamabe District, Kazusa Province'''.

During the '''Tenshō era''' (1573–1592), the shrine buildings were reconstructed. It is said that funding was provided by the '''Kiso clan''', which had been granted land in Ashido, Shimōsa Province, due to their ties with the shrine’s priests. However, the buildings were later destroyed in a '''typhoon''', and the current '''main hall''' was rebuilt in '''Shōtoku 1 (1711)'''. The structure retains '''Momoyama-period architectural elements''' and features '''karahafu''' and '''chidori-hafu''' gables, '''irimoya-zukuri''' roofing, and '''thatched cypress bark roofing'''. The extensive use of '''flat-relief carvings''' makes it a '''unique example''' of its kind and has earned it designation as a '''Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property'''. The worship hall contains a '''wooden plaque from Hōreki 11 (1761)'''.

In '''Kyōhō 2 (1717)''', the '''Shingikan (Shinto Affairs Office)''' granted the shrine the Rank of '''Shōichii (Senior First Rank)'''. In '''Meiji 5 (1872)''', the shrine’s original name, '''Goshō Daigongen''', was changed to '''Goshō Shrine'''. It was later designated a '''Gōsha (district shrine)''' in '''Taishō 11 (1922)'''.

== Rituals & Events ==
The '''Jūnimen Kagura''' (Twelve-Faced Kagura) is a '''well-known ritual dance''' performed at the shrine '''every third Sunday in February'''. According to legend, the tradition began in '''1746''' when '''Hōreki 3rd-year Daimyō Hōriya Masasuke''' donated '''40 koku of rice''' to the shrine to celebrate his return to '''Sakura''', his family’s ancestral domain. In return, local shrine priests performed a '''kagura''' at Goshō Shrine, praying for the prosperity of the '''Horita clan''' and the safety of the local villagers.

== Folk Beliefs ==
There is a local belief that '''standing in the wind during Jūnimen Kagura protects against colds and illness'''.  

The '''main hall underwent major renovations''', and '''photographs of the restored structure can be viewed via the external links below'''.  

During the renovations, '''the enshrined deity was transported by a person named Akiba Hiroshi'''.

== Related Sites & Shrines ==
Goshō Shrine oversees '''Yasashi Shrine''' (箭挿神社), located in the '''center of Kujūkuri Beach'''. Despite its name, it is a '''small wayside shrine rather than a full-fledged shrine complex'''.  

Nearby shrines include:  
* '''Shisha Shrine (Yokoshibahikari)''' (四社神社) – About '''2 km northeast'''.  
* '''Ōmiya Shrine (Sanmu)''' (大宮神社) – About '''3 km southwest'''.  

== Access ==
* '''Train:'''  
** Take the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} '''Sōbu Main Line''' to '''{{ill|Yokoshiba Station|en|Yokoshiba Station|qid=Q5363081|lt=Yokoshiba Station|1=Yokoshiba Station}}''', then take the '''Chiba Kōtsū bus''' (Hasunuma loop), alighting at '''Goshō Shrine'''.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
- *Chiba Prefecture Place Names* (日本歴史地名大系 12), Heibonsha, 1996. ISBN 978-4-582-49012-1.  
- *Nationwide Shrine Name Directory (Vol. 1)*, Nationwide Shrine Name Directory Publishing Association, Shigaku Center, 1977.  

== External Links ==
* [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001470.html Sanmu City Official Website – Cultural Properties – Goshō Shrine Main Hall]  
* [https://www.city.sammu.lg.jp/page/page001493.html (Same) – Yasashi Shrine Plaque & Minamoto no Yoritomo Statue]  
* [http://nire.main.jp/rouman/fudoki/16tiba13.htm Mythical Forest – Folk Chronicles – Chiba Prefecture – Yoritomo of Bōsō]  

==Here is a list of regional [[Sōja shrine|Sōja]] including this one==

* {{ill|Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|ja|赤坂上之山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaka Kaminoyama Shrine|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Unakorowake Shrine|ja|宇奈己呂和気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unakorowake Shrine|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|ja|大野神社 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Shrine (Ritto City)|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|ja|雄神神社 (砺波市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogami Shrine (Tonami City)|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Kainan Shrine|ja|海南神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kainan Shrine|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Katsushika Shrine|ja|葛飾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsushika Shrine|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Katsube Shrine|ja|勝部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsube Shrine|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Mukuhashi Sōja|ja|椋橋総社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukuhashi Sōja|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Kōdai-jingū|ja|皇大神宮 (藤沢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōdai-jingū|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|ja|五所神社 (山武市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosho Shrine (Sammu City)|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Sakata Shinmei Shrine|ja|坂田神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Shinkai Sansha Shrine|ja|新海三社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai Sansha Shrine|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Zama Shrine (Daito City)|ja|坐摩神社 (大東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zama Shrine (Daito City)|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Kenda Suga Shrine|ja|健田須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenda Suga Shrine|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|橘樹神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine (Kawasaki City)|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Torakashi Shrine|ja|虎柏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torakashi Shrine|qq=}} 
* {{ill|Nagao Shrine|en|Nagao Shrine|qid=Q6958481|lt=Nagao Shrine|1=Nagao Shrine}} 
* {{ill|Nanba Shrine|ja|難波神社|1=Nanba Shrine|de|Namba-Schrein|fr|Namba-jinja|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanba Shrine|qq=}} (may have historically been a provincal one)
* {{ill|Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|ja|新屋坐天照御魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiya ni Masu Amateru Mitama Shrine|qq=}}
* {{ill|Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|lt=Nogi Shrine|ja|能義神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Shrine (Tokyo)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|qid=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine|1=Hotaka Shrine}}
* {{ill|Washinomiya Shrine|en|Washinomiya Shrine|qid=Q611108|lt=Washinomiya Shrine|1=Washinomiya Shrine}}
